ralph_core_truths.what_is_it: Ralph Workflow is the operating system for autonomous coding: a free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator.
ralph_core_truths.who_is_it_for: Developers and technical teams doing ambitious software work that benefits from a structured workflow instead of a chat session.
ralph_core_truths.why_different: It keeps a simple Ralph-loop core, then composes that core into planning, development, verification, and broader workflow loops with strong defaults.
ralph_core_truths.why_now: You can use the default workflow as-is today, or build your own workflow on top without giving up control of your tools or process.

discovered_candidates.title: Claude Code Routines: Anthropic's Answer to Unattended Dev Automation
discovered_candidates.relevance: Direct competitor framing — Claude Code Routines are Anthropic's cloud-hosted unattended automation platform. Key differentiator for Ralph: Ralph is local-first, vendor-neutral, composable. Routines lock you into Anthropic infra and cloud pricing.
discovered_candidates.action: Potential backlink target or comparison content

discovered_candidates.title: Claude Code in 2026: Practical End-to-End SDLC Workflow
discovered_candidates.relevance: Comprehensive SDLC integration guide — shows how Claude Code is being positioned as end-to-end tool. Ralph's planning→build→verify loop maps onto the same SDLC stages but is tool-agnostic.
discovered_candidates.action: Blog idea: 'Why a loop framework beats any single agent tool for SDLC automation'

discovered_candidates.title: Agentic Coding in 2026: Why Multi-Agent Workflows Are Becoming the New Dev Stack
discovered_candidates.relevance: Strong framing of multi-agent orchestration as the new normal. Mentions pipeline pattern (plan→build→verify) directly. Ralph's actual architecture maps perfectly onto this narrative.
discovered_candidates.action: Potential target for Ralph mention / comparison. Blog: 'The four orchestration patterns Ralph Workflow handles natively'

discovered_candidates.title: The Complete Guide to Running AI Coding Agents Overnight
discovered_candidates.relevance: Comprehensive guide on overnight unattended runs. Discusses task boards, orchestrators, isolated worktrees, watchdogs. Direct competitor mention (amux.io). Ralph solves the same problem with a different architecture.
discovered_candidates.action: Potential backlink opportunity if Ralph can be mentioned as alternative. Blog content: 'What amux' architecture gets right and where a loop framework is more reliable'

discovered_candidates.title: Claude Code Routines and Auto Mode: Anthropic's Bet on Unattended AI Development
discovered_candidates.relevance: Detailed analysis of Auto Mode two-stage classifier and Routines cloud infra. Ralph's advantage: no approval fatigue problem when running headless loop-based workflow; Ralph simply doesn't prompt at all during unattended runs.
discovered_candidates.action: Angle for comparison content: 'Anthropic needs a two-stage classifier — Ralph just needs a spec. Here's why.'

discovered_candidates.title: Inside the Agentic Loop: Deep Technical Dive into AI Coding Agents (dev.to)
discovered_candidates.relevance: Excellent technical deep dive on agentic loop architecture. Mentions Uber's 25% Claude Code commits. Strong framing for loop-based vs. prompt-based approaches. Ralph's core loop concept is exactly what this article calls 'the agentic loop'.
discovered_candidates.action: Potential comment/link mention opportunity. Blog: 'The agentic loop is the architecture — Ralph Workflow is the framework'

discovered_candidates.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration: 4 Patterns That Actually Work
discovered_candidates.relevance: Covers Pipeline, Router, Orchestrator-Worker, Evaluator-Optimizer patterns. Ralph's plan→build→verify is a hybrid Pipeline + Evaluator-Optimizer. Directly comparable.
discovered_candidates.action: Blog content: 'Ralph Workflow implements three of the four orchestration patterns with one TOML file'

discovered_candidates.title: How to Run Claude Code Unattended — Ralph Workflow (own blog)
discovered_candidates.relevance: Own content — showed up in search results. Confirms the blog post is ranking for 'Claude Code unattended' queries. Good signal.

discovered_candidates.title: 10 Best AI Coding Agents in 2026 — Complete Guide (openagents.org)
discovered_candidates.relevance: Comprehensive comparison of Claude Code, Codex CLI, Aider, Cursor, Windsurf, etc. Does NOT mention Ralph Workflow. Gap in the comparison landscape.
discovered_candidates.action: Outreach opportunity: get Ralph added to this comparison. Blog post: 'Why the 10 best AI coding agents list is missing the most important one'

discovered_candidates.title: Claude Code Auto Mode Goes GA: 30-to-60-Minute Unattended Coding Is Now Normal
discovered_candidates.relevance: Key industry signal — headline says 30-60 minute unattended runs are benchmark norm. Ralph already does overnight multi-hour runs with checkpoint/resume. Ralph's actual capability exceeds the current industry bar.
discovered_candidates.action: Great comparison angle: '30 minutes vs. overnight — why Ralph's loop architecture matters for real unattended work'

discovered_candidates.title: amux.io — Agent orchestration with tmux + watchdog
discovered_candidates.relevance: Direct competitor in the overnight-agent-orchestration space. Amux wraps tmux with an orchestrator, REST API, dashboard. Ralph is structurally different (loop-based vs. tmux-pane-based).

reuse_contract.purpose: Shared market, competitor, and content opportunity findings for site updates, positioning checks, and marketing tactics.
